normalized_title: Development Control Policy 1.7 General Road Planning
version_date: June 1998 (Commission formally revised the policy in June 1998)
scope_summary: WAPC policy consolidating road planning requirements that apply generally to all roads (other than those covered by DC 2.6 Residential Road Planning). It sets out when land for road widening must be ceded free of cost at subdivision/development, requirements for upgrading and constructing existing roads, contributions for major (primary and district distributor) roads, rights-of-way, closure of private streets, road reserves as service corridors for public utilities (including sewer location rules for small residential lots), and corner truncation standards by functional road class.

key_numeric_standards.applies_to: intersections involving district distributor roads (except industrial/commercial areas)
district distributor intersections in industrial areas
local distributor roads, subject to local government agreement

key_numeric_standards.unit: m (lesser dimension acceptable with local government and servicing authority agreement)

key_numeric_standards.applies_to: intersections of two private streets or a private street with a public street

key_numeric_standards.topic: truncation - unclassified roads / non-residential areas minimum
key_numeric_standards.unit: m (minimum, unless local government agrees to reduction)
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: roads not fitting the functional road classification and non-residential (industrial, commercial, rural) areas

key_numeric_standards.topic: small lot subdivision threshold for sewers in road reserves
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: small lot subdivisions - Water Corporation policy requires sewers within road reserves wherever possible

key_numeric_standards.topic: minimum net lot area where sewer easement within lot
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: land coded R20 and below; for land coded above R20, not less than the minimum lot size in the Residential Planning Codes
subdivider construction contributions for district distributor roads (earthworks for whole reserve plus one two-lane carriageway and drainage)

definitions: truncation (cl 3.7.1: a line joining the points equidistant from the intersection of the street reserve boundaries)
private streets / rights-of-way (s3.4-3.5: narrow laneways at the side or rear of lots in established areas)

cross_references.cited: Policy DC 1.4 Functional Road Classification for Planning
Planning Bulletin No. 18 Developer Contributions for Infrastructure

quality_flags: rotated sidebar text ('GENERAL ROAD PLANNING POLICY NO. DC 1.7') extracted one character per line, repeated before each page - scan/layout artifact
two-column interleaving in places (e.g. ss 3.2/3.3 and 3.4/3.6 merged across columns), text recoverable

1998 policy: cites the repealed Town Planning and Development Act 1928 and notes Liveable Neighbourhoods as a trial code - statutory references are dated though the policy remains the current published version
